name: Tadalafil Cardiovascular, Endothelial Function, And Blood Pressure Baseline Search

search_queries.query: tadalafil endothelial function randomized trial flow-mediated dilation men erectile dysfunction
search_queries.result_count: Targeted web/PubMed result set; key PMIDs 15661417, 16943794, 22790642, 30638829.

search_queries.query: tadalafil blood pressure vascular stiffness obesity randomized placebo
tadalafil type 2 diabetes microcirculation endothelial function randomized placebo MAKROTAD
phosphodiesterase type 5 inhibitors vascular function systematic review meta-analysis randomized controlled trials blood pressure FMD
tadalafil therapy pulmonary arterial hypertension PHIRST randomized placebo 40 mg
cardiovascular effects of tadalafil blood pressure healthy male subjects 26 weeks
tadalafil cerebral small vessel disease blood pressure randomized ETLAS-2

inclusion_criteria: Human randomized trial, clinical pharmacology, pooled safety, or systematic-review evidence involving tadalafil or clearly marked PDE5 class vascular effects.

Outcomes include endothelial function, flow-mediated dilation, blood pressure, arterial stiffness, microcirculation, vascular biomarkers, pulmonary vascular disease outcomes, cardiovascular events, or hemodynamic safety.
Preference for tadalafil-specific evidence; class-level evidence included only as adjacent plausibility with agent-directness limits.
Include safety and interaction records when needed to frame blood-pressure lowering and vascular claims.
exclusion_criteria: Animal, in vitro, or mechanistic-only papers without human cardiovascular or vascular endpoints for this baseline.
ED efficacy trials without endothelial, blood-pressure, hemodynamic, or cardiovascular safety endpoints.
Pulmonary-hypertension sources without tadalafil-specific randomized or structured follow-up evidence.
Non-tadalafil PDE5 trials unless synthesized at class level and explicitly bounded.
dedupe_method: PMID/DOI dedupe plus existing-record reuse for Porst 2012, ETLAS-2 2025, and safety records.

screening_counts.records_identified: At least 65 across targeted PubMed/web queries.
screening_counts.full_text_reviewed: 0 full texts; PubMed abstracts and metadata were used, with PMC availability noted where present.

screening_decisions.title: Chronic treatment with tadalafil improves endothelial function in men with increased cardiovascular risk.
screening_decisions.reason: Small tadalafil-specific randomized trial with quantified FMD and endothelial biomarker improvements in cardiovascular-risk men.

screening_decisions.title: Relationship between chronic tadalafil administration and improvement of endothelial function in men with erectile dysfunction: a pilot study.
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant open-label randomized crossover ED pilot, but not staged in this first baseline because Rosano 2005 and later blinded/null ED trials provide a clearer positive/null contrast.

screening_decisions.title: Effects of once-daily tadalafil on treatment satisfaction, psychosocial outcomes, spontaneous erections, and measures of endothelial function in men with erectile dysfunction but naive to phosphodiesterase type 5 inhibitors.
screening_decisions.reason: Existing source directly reports no significant endothelial dysfunction parameter differences after 12 weeks of common low-dose daily tadalafil in ED.

screening_decisions.title: Endothelial Dysfunction in Patients With Erectile Dysfunction: A Double-Blind, Randomized-Control Trial Using Tadalafil.
screening_decisions.reason: Tadalafil-specific double-blind placebo-controlled FMD trial showing no between-group FMD advantage and more adverse events.

screening_decisions.title: Tadalafil Improves Haemodynamics and Arterial Stiffness but Not Flow-Mediated Dilation in Grade 1 Obesity.
screening_decisions.reason: Tadalafil-specific acute placebo-controlled evidence for BP and arterial stiffness effects outside ED, with null FMD.

screening_decisions.title: Feasibility of high-dose tadalafil and effects on insulin resistance in well-controlled patients with type 2 diabetes (MAKROTAD).
screening_decisions.reason: Tadalafil-specific randomized crossover cardiometabolic trial with microcirculation/endothelial-function signal, neutral primary insulin-resistance endpoint, and high-dose tolerability caveats.

screening_decisions.title: Associations between phosphodiesterase type 5 inhibitors and vascular function: a systematic review and meta-analysis on randomized-controlled trials.
screening_decisions.reason: Current PDE5 class-level synthesis of RCT vascular surrogate outcomes; useful adjacent evidence when class-transfer limits remain explicit.

screening_decisions.title: Tadalafil therapy for pulmonary arterial hypertension.
screening_decisions.reason: Landmark tadalafil-specific PAH randomized trial establishing disease-specific pulmonary vascular benefit but low directness for healthy systemic cardiovascular prevention.

screening_decisions.title: Cardiovascular effects of tadalafil.
screening_decisions.reason: Tadalafil-specific pooled cardiovascular safety/BP source with healthy-subject BP and nitrate-interaction context.

screening_decisions.title: Tadalafil Treatment in Patients With Cerebral Small Vessel Disease: The ETLAS-2 Randomized Clinical Trial.
screening_decisions.reason: Existing source provides a newer non-ED/BPH tadalafil trial with explicit no blood-pressure difference and tolerability signal.

screening_decisions.title: Analysis of integrated clinical safety data of tadalafil in patients receiving concomitant antihypertensive medications.
screening_decisions.reason: Already published safety source reused for antihypertensive and MACE boundary context; no new cardiovascular-efficacy record staged.

screening_summary: The targeted baseline found tadalafil-specific human evidence for vascular surrogate effects, but no direct trial showing long-term cardiovascular event reduction, longevity, or preventive benefit in mostly healthy men age 30 to 50. Positive signals are strongest for surrogate endothelial markers in small cardiovascular-risk or metabolic disease studies, acute blood-pressure/arterial-stiffness changes in obesity, and disease-specific PAH outcomes at 40 mg daily. Null or limiting evidence includes later ED trials with no between-group endothelial advantage and ETLAS-2 with no blood-pressure difference plus more adverse events. A current PDE5 class meta-analysis supports vascular plausibility but remains adjacent rather than tadalafil-specific proof.
limitations: Targeted baseline, not a full systematic review.
Most extraction is abstract-level; full text is needed before quantitative synthesis of endothelial/microcirculation subdomains.
No direct healthy male 30-50 cardiovascular prevention trial was identified.
Most endpoints are surrogate vascular markers, not MI, stroke, mortality, or validated healthspan outcomes.
PAH evidence is disease-specific and high-dose; it should not be generalized to healthy systemic cardiovascular health.
